name: Carne Asada
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Top Round Steak Or Boneless
12 Flour Tortillas, 5 to 8 Inches In Diameter
2 medium Onions, Sliced Paper Thin or Chopped
4 oz Whole Green Chilies, Cut Into Strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Place steak in a plastic bag or non-metal baking dish.
2. In a small bowl, combine marinade ingredients: red wine vinegar, oil, sage leaves, summer savory, salt, dry mustard, and paprika.
3. Pour the marinade over the steak, turning to coat evenly. Seal the bag or cover the dish; marinate for at least 6 hours or overnight in the refrigerator, turning once or twice.
4. When ready to barbecue, drain the meat, reserving the marinade in a small saucepan. Add steak sauce to the marinade and blend well.
5. Heat on the grill. Place the steak 4 to 6 inches from medium-hot coals. Cook for 30 to 40 minutes, turning once, or until desired doneness, brushing occasionally with marinade.
6. Meanwhile, heat foil-wrapped tortillas on the grill until thoroughly heated and steaming. Wrap in a cloth napkin or towel to keep warm.
7. To serve, cut steak across grain into thin slices. Spoon any remaining marinade over slices.
8. Arrange steak, warmed tortillas, onions, chilies, softened butter, salsa, and guacamole on a large platter.
9. Spread butter on the tortilla and top with meat and any combination of vegetables or sauce. Roll up to eat.
Note: Be sure to heat basting sauce thoroughly to ensure safety for use as a sauce at the table.
